name: ds-badge
description: Inline status chip. Text-only content, three semantic variants. Non-interactive by design.

properties.name: variant
properties.description: Semantic intent; drives foreground color only.

slots.name: default
slots.description: Badge text. Text only, matching ds-button's default-slot pattern for icon-adjacent labels.

constraints.rationale: No primitive references, no raw values.
Every variant's foreground/background pairing must independently clear AA in both contexts; a badge is never a focus stop.
Adaptive class: API drift is structural drift.

provenance.author: ai
provenance.justification: Design decision: variant changes foreground color only (against a constant --sem-color-surface-bg or --sem-color-emphasis-bg backdrop) rather than swapping background color per variant. Rejected alternative: a colored background per variant (e.g. a solid green pill for 'success'), which is the more common badge pattern. Rejected for this POC because it would require three new background/foreground contrast pairs, none of which are declared in tokens.json's ds.contrastPair extensions -- adding them is a tokens.json edit, which is out of scope for an AI-authored definition (CLAUDE.md restricts spec edits to the human-approved ds-button change only). Reusing --sem-color-feedback-success / --sem-color-feedback-error / --sem-color-emphasis-fg, all of which already declare a contrastPair against a background this component consumes, keeps the badge inside the existing token wall with real, checked contrast rather than an unchecked new surface.
